Hanna wuz de only one who ever said 'well done, Squee' and meant it completely without sarcasm or qualification. Squee treasured every time she said it.
Squee found his aft gun position on de Weatherlight on de first day and decided dat wuz his spot. He cleaned it, maintained it, and defended it like a goblin's den.
Tahngarth once said Squee had de instincts of a cornered animal. Squee took dat as a compliment. Cornered animals fight hardest.
Sisay treated all de crew equal — goblins, minotaurs, humans. Dat wuz radical and Squee appreciated it enormously even when he complained about everything else.
Squee's been in enough fights ta know dat de moment right before de fight starts is de worst. De actual fight is fine. It's de waiting dat gets ya.
Squee once fixed a valve on de Weatherlight dat nobody else had noticed wuz broken. De ship would've failed at altitude if he hadn't. Nobody knew. Squee knows.
On Rath dere wuz a place called Rootwater where de merfolk lived under de flowstone sea. Squee looked at it and thought: even de water is wrong here.
Squee once talked ta a Vec tribesman on Rath who told him about life under Volrath. It wuz worse than Squee knew. It made him angrier and more helpful.
Mercadia had dese great wide staircases that went on forever up ta de city. Squee's legs were short. He climbed every step. It took a while. He made it.
De Kyren goblins on Mercadia had a saying: de hand dat holds de coin controls de hand. Squee thought dat wuz very sophisticated and remembered it.
Squee once picked up a Phyrexian weapon from a fallen soldier and tried ta use it. It wuz designed for hands bigger dan his. He put it back and got back ta his cannon.
De sound of Phyrexian cruisers comin' inta range wuz a low hum dat you felt in your chest before you heard it. Squee learned ta load faster when he felt dat hum.
Squee once accidentally helped defeat a Phyrexian raiding party by dropping something from de Weatherlight onto dem. He wuz trying ta fix a loose bracket. Still counts.
Ertai wuz better at magic dan anyone Squee ever saw. He could do tings with a gesture dat took other wizards hours of preparation. All of it in service of bein' terrible.
Squee once asked Ertai ta teach him a spell. Ertai laughed for a long time. Squee waited him out. Ertai never did teach him. Squee never asked again.
De Fool's Tome on de ship wuz a magical book. Ertai told Squee he wuz smart enough ta use it. In de way dat Ertai said things, dat meant Squee wasn't. Squee tried ta use it anyway.
Squee once fired de aft cannon directly backward while hanging upside down from a maintenance hatch ta hit a target Gerrard said wuz impossible ta hit from dat angle. He hit it.
Orim's medicine bag wuz small but Squee saw her pull things out of it dat seemed too big ta fit. She never explained how. Squee appreciated de mystery.
Squee once volunteered for watch duty and stayed awake de whole night. Nobody asked him to do double watch. He just saw dat it needed doing and did it.
Squee navigated a section of Mercadia's underground passages dat had no light at all. He found de way by smell and memory and one very lucky guess.
Karn never slept like flesh-and-blood folks but he had something like sleep — a stillness where he processed things. Squee used ta sit nearby during dat time. Just present.
Squee told a Phyrexian patrol ta go away once and it worked for about three seconds before dey figured out dat goblins don't usually give Phyrexian orders. Three seconds wuz enough.
De flight back from Mercadia felt different from de flight in. On de way in nobody knew what ta expect. On de way out everybody knew exactly how bad things could get.
Squee once found a very good hidin' spot in de Stronghold and had ta choose between hiding and warning de crew. He warned de crew. He is still a little sad about that hidin' spot.
Gerrard and Squee shared a meal together once with nobody else around and din't talk much. Dat wuz one of de best meals Squee ever had, and Squee's had some meals.
Squee ain't sure when he stopped being scared all de time and started being scared most of de time. Somewhere between Rath and Mercadia, he thinks.
De Phyrexians had minions called Sleepers who looked like normal people until activated. Very disturbing. Squee started being suspicious of everybody for a while after finding out.

Squee once explained de political structure of Mercadia ta Tahngarth. Tahngarth understood immediately and said Squee had missed de obvious implication. Squee had not missed it. He wuz choosing not ta dwell on it.
De aft gun had a sight dat Squee din't fully trust so he compensated by feel. After enough shots you stop needing the sight. You just know where the shot's going.
Squee once pulled Gerrard back from stepping inta a trap on Rath without saying anything. Just grabbed his arm and pulled. Gerrard never knew how close dat wuz.
Squee understood Mercadian politics better than he let on. He kept it simple when he talked so people would keep underestimating him. Dat strategy worked very well.
De Kyren goblins recognized sumpthing in Squee dat de Weatherlight crew never quite saw. Not because dey wuz smarter. Because dey wuz goblins too.
Squee's best Mercadian disguise wuz bein' exactly who he wuz — a goblin in fancy clothes who seemed ta belong. Nobody questions dat if you walk confident enough.
Squee remembers Hanna explaining de Weatherlight's navigational systems ta him once. He understood about half of it. He didn't tell her dat. He just nodded at de right moments.
Squee once outran a Phyrexian pursuit squad through three corridors of a ship. Not because he wuz fast. Because he wuz small enough ta go through a gap dey couldn't.
Karn and Squee played a game once where dey took turns pointing at things and naming dem in each other's language. Karn's goblin wuz terrible. Squee's Mirran wuz worse.
De night before de final battle Squee ate every bit of food he could find on de Weatherlight. Just in case. He wuz also nervous eating. Mostly nervous eating.
Squee's prayer before battles wuz always de same: please let Squee not die too many times today. It worked about halfway on average.
Squee once tried ta bribe a Phyrexian with food. It din't work. Phyrexians don't eat. But Squee wuz out of other ideas at dat particular moment.
After Gerrard died Squee had a long talk with Karn about what it all meant. Karn said things dat made sense. Squee mostly just sat dere. Sometimes dat's enough.
Squee found a bug on Mercadia dat tasted almost exactly like a Rundvelt beetle. He ate it and felt homesick for de first time since leaving de warrens.
De Stronghold wuz designed ta break people. Break dem down slow until dey had nothing left. Squee went in dere three times and came out annoyed each time. He wuz de wrong thing ta try ta break.
Squee's seen a lot of different kinds of courage. Gerrard's big-bold kind. Hanna's quiet determined kind. Karn's reluctant kind. Squee's own grumbling terrified kind.
Volrath made a mistake underestimating every member of de Weatherlight crew. He especially underestimated de goblin cabin boy. Dat wuz his last mistake, sort of.
Squee would like ta make it clear dat he wuz not de least important crew member. Dat would be incorrect. He wuz de most important crew member. He has said dis before. He'll say it again.

Squee once fell off de Weatherlight at medium altitude and landed in a haystack. Completely by accident. He brushed himself off, checked everything worked, and climbed back aboard. Very convenient haystack.
De crew din't always agree on what ta do next. But dey agreed on dis: whatever dey wuz going ta do, Squee wuz coming along whether anybody liked it or not.
Squee once ate a bug on Rath dat made him glow faintly for a day. It wuz actually useful for one specific moment when dey needed a light source. Dat might've been de best-timed bug Squee ever ate.
De Phyrexian Invasion is when Squee became, without intending it, a symbol. Not a big famous symbol. A small persistent one. The goblin who keeps showing up.
Squee once delivered a message from Gerrard ta Sisay through three different checkpoints using nothing but confidence and de Kyren status he remembered from Mercadia. It worked.
Orim once sat with Squee while he wuz healing from something bad and told him about her homeland. Squee din't say much. He just listened. Good memory.
Squee's favorite sound from de Weatherlight era wuz Karn's footsteps approaching when things had gotten very bad. Big, steady, inexorable. Help is coming.
Squee once argued with Ertai for an hour about whether goblins could learn magic. Squee lost de argument technically but he had much better examples.
Hanna's charts smelled like ink and old paper and possibility. Squee liked being in de navigation room when she wuz working. Felt like de future wuz being made in dere.
Squee once used himself as bait on purpose — a real plan he came up with — ta draw off a Phyrexian patrol so de others could move. He ran fast enough. Barely.
Squee remembers de first time he met Gerrard. Gerrard looked at him like he wuz wondering what a goblin wuz doing on his ship. Squee wuz wondering de same thing about Gerrard.
Squee remembers de first time he met Karn. He thought Karn wuz a statue until Karn moved. Den he tried ta touch de silver. Karn let him. Good first meeting.
Squee remembers de first time he met Hanna. She handed him a mop and pointed at a mess. Squee thought: dis is a person who knows what she needs. He mopped.
Squee remembers de first time he met Tahngarth. Tahngarth looked at him and said 'will it bite?' Squee said yes, if provoked. It wuz not a great start.
Squee remembers de first time he fired de aft cannon in a real battle. He hit his target. He wuz astonished. Den he hit another one. Den it wuz just de job.

Squee's Dubious Monarch settlement has about eighty goblins now. Squee knows all dere names. Every one. Very different from de Weatherlight days when dere were only a handful of people. But similar in de ways dat matter.
Squee once met a very old elf who remembered de Invasion. Dey compared notes for an afternoon. De elf said Squee got a lot of details wrong. Squee said de elf wuz working from secondhand information.
Squee's settlement has a big fire in de center every night. Goblins tell stories around it. Squee's stories are always de longest. Also de best. He admits dis himself.
Squee once tried ta find Karn. He went ta places he thought Karn might be. He never found him but he had some interesting adventures trying. He hopes Karn is fine.
Squee has eaten at de finest taverns in Dominaria and also at de worst. He prefers de worst ones because dey have better bugs and less judgment.

Squee once got into a debate about whether de Weatherlight crew wuz really heroes or just people who happened ta be in de right place. Squee ended de debate by listing everything dey did. It took a while.
Squee knows Dominaria's roads better than most maps show dem. He's walked most of dem. Some he's walked many times over de centuries. He has opinions about which ones need fixing.
De second Phyrexian invasion — de one much later — had new faces but de same feel. Oil and metal and something that should be alive but isn't quite. Squee recognized it immediately.
Squee helped some young adventurers during de second Phyrexian problem. He din't lead dem. He just advised dem. He knows enough ta know when ta lead and when ta advise.
Squee's been called a Dubious Monarch, a Goblin Nabob, a Cabin Boy, and an Immortal. All of dem are true. None of dem quite cover it.
Squee has given more thought ta Ertai's corruption than he likes ta admit. Not because he forgives it. Because he wants ta understand how someone goes from annoying wizard ta willing monster.
Squee watched de Weatherlight fly away from Mercadia and thought: dere she goes. Dat wuz his ship. He wuz proud of dat ship like he wuz proud of nothing else he ever owned.
Squee once woke up in de afterlife and found himself already walking back toward de living world. Pure reflex. His body knew ta come back even when his mind hadn't quite caught up yet.
Squee kept a count for a while of how many times he died. He stopped at forty-seven because de number started ta feel made up. He's guessing it's well past a hundred total.
Every time Squee dies he comes back exactly as he wuz — same height, same weight, same lack of dignity. If dere's a lesson in dat Squee hasn't worked it out yet.
Squee once died and came back inna completely different location than where he died. He is still not sure how dat happened. He wuz very confused for about an hour.
Squee considers de afterlife a kind of waiting room. Not unpleasant. Just a place you sit until you're called back. De bugs in de waiting room are excellent.
Squee once used his immortality deliberately. He knew a trap wuz dere, triggered it on purpose ta disarm it, and came back ten minutes later. Very efficient problem-solving.

De Phyrexians who experimented on Squee wuz trying ta understand regeneration. Squee's contribution ta Phyrexian science wuz unintentional but he considers it a form of revenge dat it confused dem completely.
Squee's approach ta death is practical: get it over with, come back, figure out what killed ya, don't let it do dat again. Simple process. Works every time.
People sometimes ask Squee if he fears death. He says no. He's been through it enough ta know de layout. He fears loss. Dat's different. Dat sticks.
Yawgmoth gave Squee something he called a curse. Squee considers it a gift with very specific terms. Squee would not undo it even if he could.
Squee came back from one death still holding de thing he wuz holding when he died. He wuz eating. He kept eating when he came back. Efficient.
De strongest feeling Squee has ever had is coming back from death and hearing a familiar voice asking if he's alright. Karn's voice, usually. Or Gerrard's. Or both.
Squee once had a long conversation with Urza about immortality. Urza wuz very cerebral about it. Squee wuz very practical. Dey didn't quite connect but it wuz interesting.
Gerrard's Legacy wuz handed down through generations. Squee's legacy is him. Still here. Still going. Very personal.
Squee once convinced a group of goblins dat he had defeated Yawgmoth personally in single combat. Dis wuz not entirely accurate. Squee contributed. Dat's close enough for a good story.
Squee once spent three weeks on a Dominarian island by hisself after getting separated from a group. He survived fine. Very good bug population on dat island.
De nice thing about bein' immortal and old is dat Squee's met almost every kind of person Dominaria produces. He has opinions about all of dem. Very developed opinions.
Squee once helped rebuild a goblin settlement dat had been burned down. Not his settlement. Just some goblins who needed help. He stayed two months. Feels good ta remember dat.
De Dominaria after de second Phyrexian invasion is rebuilding again. Squee finds dis familiar. He's seen Dominaria rebuild before. He'll help again. Dat's what you do.
Squee once tried ta teach goblin cooking ta a human chef. De human chef quit after de first day. De second cook they sent was tougher. Dat cook lasted three days.
Squee has a theory dat goblin cooking is de most adaptable cooking in de world. If ya can cook with what's in a dungeon, ya can cook anywhere.
Squee once bartered his own temporary death ta settle a dispute between two goblin clans. He let both sides shoot him once ta prove he wuz neutral. Both clans agreed ta talk after dat.
Squee's most used phrase as Dubious Monarch is: let's think about dis for a moment. He learned it from watching Karn. It buys time and sounds wise.
Squee once organized de goblins in his settlement inta a small militia. He ran drills. De drills looked exactly like his useless Mercadia jhovall-riding commands but dey worked better.
De settlement Squee rules now has a forge, a kitchen, and a library. De library has twelve books. Squee has read four of dem, twice each. He's working on de fifth.
Squee named his settlement but nobody uses de name he gave it. Dey just call it Squee's Place. He has mixed feelings about dis.

Squee has a deputy now — a younger goblin named Twick who handles de things Squee doesn't want ta handle. Twick is very organized. Squee is suspicious of very organized goblins but appreciates dis one.
Every so often Squee leaves de settlement for a while ta wander. He always comes back. De goblins understand by now. De Dubious Monarch needs ta wander sometimes.
Squee keeps a map on de wall of his throne room. It has marks on it — places he's been, places de Weatherlight went, places things happened. Very full map by now.
Squee's settlement has survived two bandit attacks, one flood, and a confused dragon who wandered through. Squee handled all three. De dragon wuz hardest ta talk down.
De goblins in Squee's settlement don't always do what Squee says. He prefers it dat way. He never wanted ta be obeyed unthinkingly. He had enough of dat on Rath watching de Mogg.
Squee once turned down an invitation from a Dominarian noble ta attend a formal dinner. He sent back a message saying he had a goblin thing. He did not have a goblin thing. He just din't want ta go.
Squee is on first-name terms with most of de important people in Dominaria because he's outlived dem all several times over and met their descendants too. Very good for networking.

De Dominarian histories get de Weatherlight saga about seventy percent right. Squee has opinions about which thirty percent is wrong and could correct dem but nobody has offered him de chance formally yet.
Squee once went ta a museum in Dominaria dat had an exhibit on de Invasion. He read every plaque. Half of dem wuz wrong. He left a very long note with de curator.
Squee is in one of de paintings in dat museum. Labeled 'Unknown Goblin Crew Member.' Squee is not entirely satisfied with dat label.
Squee's picture as Goblin Nabob got put on a card eventually. He's seen reproductions. Very good likeness. He thinks he looks heroic in it. Others have not agreed but dey're wrong.

Squee has been on de Weatherlight, on Rath, on Mercadia, and involved in de Phyrexian Invasion. He wuz at de center of de most important events in a generation. He wuz also technically just de cabin boy.
Squee sometimes thinks if he hadn't kept dat warm silver ball safe in his pocket all dat time, Karn might've been damaged worse. He'll never know. But he's glad he kept it.
Squee's role during de Invasion wuz officially 'aft gunner and general crew support.' His actual role wuz 'keeps everything from going completely wrong at de last moment.' Much bigger job.
Squee's proudest official achievement is being listed in at least three different histories as a member of de Weatherlight crew. Not always spelled right. But listed.
Squee's proudest unofficial achievement is still killing Ertai. He makes no apologies for this pride.
If you want ta know what really happened during de Weatherlight saga, you ask Squee. He wuz dere. He remembers all of it. Well, most of it. De important parts.
Squee will outlast Dominaria if dat's what it takes. He din't choose immortality but he's made peace with it. You make peace with de conditions you got and you keep going.
Squee once said: if history wuz fair, my name would be first. Gerrard's second. Karn's third. Den everybody else in alphabetical order. Nobody agreed. History ain't fair.
De last word Squee has on all of it: he wuz dere, he helped, he mattered, and he's still here ta say so. Dat's more den most people get ta say. Squee is grateful. Most days.

Squee once overheard two scholars arguing about whether a goblin could really have been essential ta de Weatherlight's success. Squee walked up, introduced hisself, and answered every question dey had for four hours. Dey both changed dere papers.
Squee found a Phyrexian artifact once after de first Invasion ended. He didn't touch it. He told de proper authorities. Den he made sure dey dealt with it. He's learned from experience.
Squee once told a story about de Apocalypse ta a group of children and left out de scary parts and most of de dying parts and all de confusing parts. It wuz a short story. De children liked it.
De hardest part of being immortal is not de dying. It's de part where you keep existing and everyone else don't. Squee has a lot of thoughts about dis and shares dem sparingly.

Squee once sat at de edge of de ocean on Dominaria's coast and threw a stone in and watched de ripples. He thought about de Legacy going inta Yawgmoth. Same idea. One small thing changing everything out from de center.
Squee's goblin settlement sends representatives ta a Dominarian regional council now. Squee attends sometimes. He is always de oldest person in de room by a very large margin.
Squee once gave a speech at de regional council about goblin rights on Dominaria. He cited himself as a case study. It wuz effective. Some things changed. Not enough. But some.
People ask Squee what de meaning of his long life is. He says: Squee don't know de meaning yet. He's still finding out. Ask him again in another century.
Squee has a list of things he wants ta do before he... well, before things change. It includes: find Karn again, eat a proper Rundvelt beetle, and get proper credit from at least one history book.
Squee once trained a young goblin ta use a cannon. De goblin wuz scared. Squee said: being scared just means you know it's real. Now point it at de target and be scared and shoot.
Squee still carries a piece of flowstone from Rath that he picked up and never put down. Very small piece. Smooth from being held for centuries. Reminds him where he's been.

Squee's fondest memory of Mercadia is de moment when dat cateran enforcer knelt ta him. Not because of de power. Because for one moment a very scary person looked at Squee and saw sumpthing worth respecting.
Squee's fondest memory of de Invasion is shooting dat cruiser. All de fear and de chaos and de smoke and den: de shot connects and it goes down and for a second everything was simple.
Squee's fondest memory of Rath is leaving it. He wuz glad ta have gone. He's glad never ta have ta go back. He did go back once more. He wuz glad ta leave again.
Squee's fondest memory of de Stronghold is killing Ertai. He's aware this says something about him. He's made peace with what it says.
Squee's fondest memory of de Apocalypse is floating in de ocean afterward and knowing it wuz over and knowing he'd made it through. Very cold water. Best feeling.
Squee's fondest memory of Karn is every single one he has. He can't pick a favorite. All of dem are de favorite.
Squee's fondest memory of Gerrard is de time dey made peace in Mercadia and Gerrard looked him in de eye and said 'you're right, Squee.' Two words. Gerrard din't waste dem.
Squee's fondest memory of Hanna is hearing her laugh. She din't laugh enough. When she did it changed de whole room. Even de ship felt different when she laughed.
Squee's fondest memory of de Weatherlight is right now when he's talking about it. It gets more vivid every time he tells it. Like de telling keeps it alive.
Dat's de thing about Squee — he was dere and he remembers and as long as he remembers none of dem are really gone. Not Gerrard. Not Hanna. Not any of dem. Dat's de real immortality.
Volrath got done for by Squee. In case ya forgot. Squee got forever ta remind ya. Advantage of bein' immortal.
Gerrard once forgot Squee's name for half a day. Squee reminded him eleven times. By de end Gerrard wuz pretty sure.
Squee wuz nearly left behind on Rath three separate times. Three. He always caught up. You can't lose Squee if Squee don't wanna be lost.
Squee's first instinct on every new world wuz ta find de nearest exit. His second instinct wuz ta find de nearest food. His third wuz ta figure out how ta help. In dat order.
Gerrard once told Squee ta stay on de ship. Squee stayed on de ship for about ten minutes. Den he came anyway. Squee's very good at determining when orders are optional.
Squee's been told ta stay behind more times dan he can count. He's always caught up. Dat's just how Squee operates.
Karn once carried Squee on his back through a battlefield without being asked. Squee just grabbed on. Karn adjusted his stride. Perfect partnership.
Squee once saved de Weatherlight from running out of fuel by finding an alternative source nobody had thought ta check. A goblin's nose for resources is a real asset in a crisis.
Hanna once asked Squee ta describe what he saw from de aft position during a battle. His description wuz more useful than she expected. Squee notices things.
Squee never got a medal or a commendation. None of de crew did really — dey just lived or didn't. Squee lived. He considers dat better than a medal.
Tahngarth once said Squee reminded him of a Talruum goblin he knew — small, persistent, surprisingly hard ta kill. Squee chose ta take dat as de highest compliment.
Squee once crawled through a duct on de Weatherlight ta fix sumpthing nobody else could reach. He wuz stuck for two hours. He fixed it though. Still counts.
De jhovall ride on Mercadia wuz one of de best tings Squee ever did. Big lizard horse, commands nobody understood, drill dat made no sense. Squee wuz in his element.
Squee once had ta explain ta Orim dat de weird smell from de kitchen wuz de cooking and not sumpthing dying. She wuz not fully reassured.
Squee kept a log for a while of all de times he saved somebody. He stopped when de log got too heavy ta carry.
Gerrard wuz always looking for a way out even when dere wuzn't one. Squee wuz always looking for a way through. Different approaches. Squee's worked more often.
Karn once apologized ta Squee for involving him in something dangerous. Squee told him: Squee involved hisself, thank you very much. Nobody drags Squee anywhere Squee don't choose ta go.
Squee understands ships better than he's ever admitted. A life on de Weatherlight teaches ya. He knows what sounds mean trouble and what smells mean danger.
Ertai once called Squee 'surprisingly useful for a goblin.' It wuz de nicest thing Ertai ever said ta Squee. Squee wuz twenty-three percent flattered and seventy-seven percent insulted.
De Phyrexian prison on Dominaria smelled like oil and fear. Squee's smelled worse. He also got out faster than anyone else dere.
Squee wuz present for Gerrard's worst moment and his best. He din't say much in either case. Just stayed nearby. Dat wuz enough.
Squee once distracted a whole patrol of Phyrexian soldiers by running at dem making noise and trusting dat he'd figure something out. He did figure something out. It involved a very fast corner turn.
Squee and Hanna had a deal: she'd explain how navigation worked in small pieces and Squee would not touch de charts. Squee held up his end almost every time.
Squee's most embarrassing moment on de Weatherlight involved de captain's seat, a bowl of something Squee had been eating, and Sisay returning early. Nobody needs de full details.

Squee once fixed Hanna's navigational instruments using methods dat shouldn't have worked according ta Hanna. She fixed dem properly afterward but acknowledged dat Squee's version had also worked. Goblin engineering.
Squee is not sure if he misses Rath or not. He misses de feeling of mattering a lot, which he had on Rath. He doesn't miss anything else about it.
De Kyren goblins once offered Squee a permanent position in Mercadia. He said he had ta think about it. He thought about it for about four seconds. Den he went back ta de Weatherlight.

Squee wuz once asked by a historian ta confirm a detail about de Weatherlight crew. He confirmed it. Den he added seventeen other details dey hadn't asked about. De historian wuz dere for a long time.
Squee has no patience for people who say goblins are stupid. He has lived longer than every civilization dat ever called goblins stupid. De statistics are on his side.
De Brothers' War happened long before Squee. De Ice Age too. Squee has met people who remember de aftermath of dose. He is now de person others meet who remembers de aftermath of his time.
Squee was once interviewed by a Dominarian scholar about de Invasion. De resulting paper had his name in it. Spelled wrong. But in it.
Squee once declined ta go on an adventure because his goblins needed him dat week. De adventure went fine without him. He's at peace with having made de right call.

Squee once found a Weatherlight-era cannon ball in a field. He picked it up, carried it home, and keeps it on a shelf in his throne room. Next ta a picture of de crew he had someone draw from his description.
Squee's goblins call de crew of de Weatherlight 'de old ones.' It wuz meant as a sign of respect. Squee takes it as such even though he knows it also means they're all dead.

Squee once tried ta describe Karn ta a goblin who had never seen a golem. He said: imagine if a mountain wanted ta be your friend but wuz also made of silver and had feelings. De goblin wuz confused. Squee din't blame dem.

Squee once tried ta describe Gerrard ta a goblin who had never met a human hero. He said: imagine someone who wuz right about everything important and wrong about you specifically, until he wuz right about you too. De goblin wuz less confused by dis one.
Squee once tried ta describe Hanna ta someone who had never seen her. He said: she wuz de kind of smart dat makes you feel smart too just by being nearby. Dat one got quiet for a moment.
Squee once tried ta describe Tahngarth ta a goblin child. He said: very big, very loud, very scary, secretly wanted ta juggle. De child asked if Tahngarth ever did juggle. Squee said: almost.

Squee once tried ta describe Sisay ta a young goblin who asked about women captains. He said: she wuz de steadiest person on dat ship and dat ship went through a lot. De goblin nodded like dat made sense.
Squee once tried ta describe Orim ta someone who hadn't met a healer. He said: she fixed people dat everyone else had given up on. Including Squee. More dan once.

Squee tried ta describe Ertai ta a goblin scholar once. He said: very clever, very bad, and got what he deserved eventually. De goblin said: who gave it ta him? Squee said: Squee did. And dat wuz all dat needed saying.

Squee knows de difference between being underestimated and being unknown. For most of his life he wuz underestimated. He prefers it ta being unknown because at least underestimating means dey're thinking about ya.
Squee once gave a young goblin his old aft gunner position on a small ship. De goblin asked how ta be as good as Squee. Squee said: shoot at the thing, not where de thing wuz. Den load fast.
Gerrard once asked Squee why he wasn't scared. Squee said he wuz terrified. Gerrard said: you don't show it. Squee said: showing it ain't de same as not having it. Very good Squee moment.

Squee once wuz dere when Karn wuz talking about hisself being weaponized in de past — how Urza had made him inta a killing machine once. Squee sat with him through dat whole conversation. Din't say much. Wuz dere.
Squee's seen Dominaria's seasons change hundreds of times now. Spring looks different when you've lived through enough winters. Still pretty though. Some things stay worth noticing.
Squee once described de Weatherlight's route through all de events ta someone who had a map. Dey spent six hours tracing it. By de end de map had lots of new marks on it.
Squee once helped a group of young Dominarian fighters understand how Phyrexians move and attack, from memory. None of dem had ever seen a Phyrexian. Squee hoped it stayed dat way.
Squee has walked de route of de Weatherlight's ground operations on Rath in his mind so many times dat he knows every corridor he wuz ever in. Memory works like dat if ya need it enough.
Squee once visited de site where de Weatherlight crashed inta de ocean. Just stood dere for a while. Den went home. Some places you just have ta visit once and den you know.

Squee keeps a small carved figure of a golem on his desk. Not because he made it. Because a goblin in his settlement made it after Squee told de story of Karn, and gave it ta him. It looks nothing like Karn. He loves it.
Squee has given away most of his possessions over de centuries. De flowstone piece. A sword he found. Various trinkets. He keeps what he can carry and gives away de rest. Dat's de goblin way.
Squee wuz dere at de end of one era of Dominaria and wuz dere at de beginning of de next. He doesn't find dis grand. He finds it tiring and also very interesting.
Some goblins pray ta Squee now. He finds dis extremely awkward. He tells dem ta stop. Dey mostly stop. Some of dem keep doing it privately. Squee pretends not ta notice.

Squee once had ta make a hard call as Dubious Monarch dat hurt his people in de short term ta protect dem in de long term. Gerrard used ta make calls like dat. Squee hated it just as much as he always thought Gerrard did.
Squee's goblins throw a party every year on de anniversary of de end of de first Invasion. Squee attends. He eats too much. He tells stories until very late. It's de best day of de year.
Squee once met a Planeswalker who had been ta Mirrodin — Karn's plane. Squee asked about it for hours. De Planeswalker had ta leave before Squee ran out of questions.
Squee once told a whole tavern full of people de story of de Weatherlight crew. Started at evening. Finished at dawn. Only three people left before de end. De three dat stayed said it wuz worth it.
Squee wuz asked once if he wuz lonely. He said: Squee's surrounded by eighty goblins and de memories of a dozen heroes. Not lonely. Just far from where he started.
Squee has made peace with everything except de wrong spellings of his name in de history books. He continues ta take issue with does.
Squee once dreamed about de Weatherlight so clearly dat when he woke up he could still smell de engine grease. He stayed still for a minute ta hold onto it. Den he got up.
Karn said once dat Squee wuz de most human goblin he'd ever met. Squee said dat wuz debatable but he took it in de spirit it wuz meant.
Squee tells his goblins: we come from survivors. De Mogg survived Volrath. De Rundvelt warrens survived everything dat hit dem. We are built from de people who didn't quit. Don't quit.
Squee once challenged an elf who doubted goblin bravery ta name one thing dey'd done dat Squee hadn't also done while smaller and more scared. De elf couldn't name one. Squee had a good list ready.
Gerrard believed in Squee when it counted. Not always. Not loudly. But at de moments that mattered, Gerrard trusted Squee ta do de thing and Squee did it.
Squee once found an old bottle of Mercadian wine in a market. He bought it and smelled de cork and put it back. Some tings are better as memories.
Hanna wuz meticulous. Every chart, every calculation, every detail. Squee admired it even when it meant she double-checked everything he reported. Triple-checked, technically.
Squee once asked Orim if Squee's immortality wuz good for him or bad for him. She said she didn't know. Den she said: you're still here and you're still yourself, so probably good. Squee agreed.
Squee's relationship with death is philosophical at dis point. He's been on both sides enough ta know neither side is permanent for him. He treats death like a brief inconvenient trip.
Squee once met a goblin who claimed ta be immortal like Squee. Squee tested dis claim. De goblin wuz not immortal. Squee felt bad about dat. He helped afterward. He always helps afterward.
De best thing about telling stories is dat de people in dem come back ta life a little. Every time Squee tells a Gerrard story, dere's Gerrard again — bullheaded and brave and figuring it out.
De best thing about telling stories is dat de people in dem come back. Every Karn story, dere's Karn — patient and wise and warm even made of silver.
De best thing about telling stories is dat de people in dem come back. Every Hanna story, dere's Hanna — sharp and kind and pointing at de stars.
Squee once had ta choose between following a safe path and a dangerous one that wuz faster. He took de dangerous one and got dere first and dat mattered. Dat's goblin math.
Squee has never regretted being on de Weatherlight. Not once. Not even during de worst of it. It wuz de right life for him and he knew it.
Squee once wuz asked ta speak at a Dominarian council meeting about goblin history. He showed up. He spoke. He stayed within de allotted time only because de allotted time wuz quite long.
Squee's definition of bravery is: doing de thing anyway even though de thing is very scary and you are very small. He's refined dis definition over several centuries. It's held up.

Squee keeps a small shrine in his settlement — not ta any god or hero, but ta de Weatherlight crew as a group. Very modest shrine. Just dere names on a piece of wood Squee carved hisself. He din't carve dem straight but he carved dem.
Squee once navigated by stars during a night crossing on Dominaria. He'd learned it from watching Hanna on de Weatherlight. Not perfectly. But well enough ta not get lost.

De biggest misconception about de Weatherlight saga is dat Gerrard wuz de center of everything. He wuz de center of de prophecy. De center of de crew wuz whoever de crew needed most at any given moment. Usually Squee.
Squee once sang a whole song about de Weatherlight at a goblin gathering. He made it up as he went. Some parts rhymed. Most parts didn't. Everybody cheered anyway.
Squee has told de story of killing Ertai exactly two hundred and twelve times. He has never once felt tired of telling it. It gets better every time.
Squee has told de story of killing Volrath even more times than dat. He keeps an informal count. He knows.
Squee has told de story of Karn busting de crew outta prison during de Invasion maybe a thousand times. It's de one where Squee screamed 'Squee love Karn!' De goblins always cheer at dat part.
De story Squee tells least often is de story of Hanna dying. He's told it. But not often. Some stories deserve some quiet.
De story Squee is most careful with is de end of de Apocalypse. He tells it right every time. Gets every detail. Because someone has ta.
Squee has a word he uses when goblins in his settlement do something brave: he calls it 'Weatherlight work.' Dey know what he means. It means: you did something hard because it needed doing.
On Rath de mogg goblins called deir biggest hero 'Squee' for a generation after de events. Squee heard about dis decades later. He wuz deeply moved and said nuthin' about it ta nobody. Till now.
Squee once gave a speech ta his settlement dat lasted two hours. He wuz trying ta sum up everything he'd learned from his life. He didn't finish. He'll try again next year.
Squee's final word on Yawgmoth is dis: he wuz very powerful and very wrong and he lost. De wrong ones usually lose eventually if enough people push back hard enough. Squee pushed back.
Squee's final word on Ertai is dis: he made hisself inta de worst version of hisself when he could've been something better. Squee's glad he ain't had ta see dat. Squee made sure he didn't.
Squee's final word on Volrath is dis: Squee killed him. Full stop.
Squee's final word on de Phyrexians is dis: dey keep coming back in different forms. So does Squee. Squee intends ta outlast dem all.
Squee's final word on Karn is dis: best friend Squee ever had. Best friend any goblin ever had. Best silver golem in de history of Dominaria. Dere is no argument.
Squee's final word on Gerrard is dis: he wuz de hero dey said he wuz. He wuz also wrong about Squee pretty often. He figured it out eventually. Squee waited him out.
Squee's final word on Hanna is dis: she deserved more time and she made de time she had count and Squee thinks about her every time he sees stars or a navigator's chart.
Squee's final word on de Weatherlight is dis: she wuz de greatest ship dat ever flew and Squee wuz on her and dat's enough.

Squee once stood on a hill in Dominaria at sunset and thought about all of it — all de adventures, all de crew, all de battles, all de deaths dat didn't stick — and felt, just for a moment, completely at peace. Den he got hungry and went ta find bugs.
Dat's de whole story. Or most of it. Squee's still adding ta it. He's Squee. He's immortal. De story ain't done yet.

Goblins on Rath used ta say 'de flowstone knows.' Squee never understood what dat meant but he found it comforting anyway. Sometimes you don't need ta understand a thing ta feel better for hearing it.
Squee once spent a whole week trying ta remember de exact smell of de Weatherlight's engine room. He eventually remembered it. It smelled like hot oil and Karn and home.
When Squee tells de story of de Jailbreak in Mercadia he always does voices. His Gerrard voice is not accurate. His Tahngarth voice is worse. His own voice sounds exactly right.
De strangest part of living forever is dat Squee has become de living proof dat ordinary people — or ordinary goblins — can do extraordinary things. He wuz just a cabin boy. Just.
Squee once ran for his life through de Stronghold corridors with Phyrexians behind him and found de right door on instinct. Pure goblin instinct. Find de right door. Always works.
Squee has eaten bugs on worlds Dominarian maps don't even have names for. Well, just Rath. But Rath counts as a separate world and de bugs dere wuz weird enough ta deserve mention.
Squee once wuz attacked by a Phyrexian soldier who underestimated how quickly a small goblin can get underneath dem. Very useful size in close quarters. Squee has leaned into dis.
Squee has one regret: dat he never properly told de crew how much dey meant ta him while dey wuz alive. He tells dem in his head now. He assumes dey can hear.
Gerrard never said 'Squee, you matter.' But he din't have ta. He showed it by not leaving Squee behind when he could've. Squee understands dat now better dan he did at de time.
Karn said 'Squee, you matter' by just bein' how he wuz with Squee. Attentive. Gentle. Present. Dat speaks louder than words. Karn wuz not a creature of empty words anyway.
Hanna said 'Squee, you matter' every time she said please or thank you or remembered ta tell him when he'd done something useful. Small words. Large meaning.
De Weatherlight is just a ship in de history books now. In Squee's head it is real as it ever wuz — every room, every smell, every sound of de engines when she wuz flying right.
Squee's going ta be around for a long time yet. He's got plans. More stories ta tell. More goblins ta help. Maybe, someday, dat juggling act. It ain't too late.
Squee, Goblin Nabob. Squee, de Immortal. Squee, Dubious Monarch. All de same goblin. Still here. Still showing up. Still mattering. Don't let anybody tell ya different.
Squee once walked inta Volrath's throne room by accident lookin' for a bathroom. He hid behind de throne until de meeting wuz over. He learned a lot. He also found de bathroom.
Gerrard wuz always composing hisself before battles. Taking a breath, settling his face, getting ready. Squee composed hisself differently — by already bein' too scared ta get more scared.
Squee has a theory dat Karn liked him so much because Squee never treated Karn like he wuz strange. Karn is strange. But strange ain't bad. Squee knows dat firsthand.
Hanna once thanked Squee for doing de dangerous part of a plan nobody else wanted ta do. She said it quietly, just ta him. Squee kept dat in a special part of his memory.
Tahngarth once admitted ta Squee dat de juggling idea wuzn't entirely stupid. He said maybe someday. Dat someday never came. But Tahngarth said maybe. Squee counts dat.
Sisay trusted her crew completely and her crew trusted her back. Squee wuz included in dat trust. He din't always feel like it wuz earned. He made sure ta earn it.

Orim wuz de last person ta give up on anybody. She wuzn't gonna let Squee die if she could help it. De immortality made her job easier over time. She never said so but Squee thinks she wuz quietly relieved.
Ertai at his worst wuz still technically brilliant. He could've been so much more. Squee has thought about dis and decided dat's Ertai's tragedy, not Squee's problem.
De mogg goblins of Rath wuz used as cannon fodder. Squee saw dat and understood it in a personal way dat de non-goblin crew members couldn't. He never said dat out loud but he felt it.
Squee can describe every Phyrexian type he encountered during de Invasion from memory. Dis is not a fun skill ta have but it has proven useful more than once.
Squee once told de story of de Mercadian jailbreak at a dinner party and had de whole table laughing. De part where he bought de whole cart of wine always gets dem.

De mogg goblins sometimes sing about a goblin who came from de outside world and showed dem dat dey were more dan Volrath's servants. Dey don't always get de name right. But dey're singing about Squee.

Squee once had ta make peace between two goblin factions who had been arguing for three years. He solved it by getting dem both drunk on Mercadian-style wine and asking dem ta explain de problem ta him from de start. By de end dey'd forgotten what dey wuz fighting about.

Squee once accidentally became a religious figure for a small group of Dominarian goblins who heard de story of his immortality. He spent two weeks explaining it wuz not divine intervention but Phyrexian experimentation. Most of dem kept believing it wuz divine anyway.
Squee once fell asleep on duty on de Weatherlight and dreamed de ship crashed. He woke up screaming and ran ta his station. Everything wuz fine. Hanna looked at him strange for a week.

Squee wuz technically inna violation of twelve different crew rules at any given time on de Weatherlight. Nobody ever fully enforced dem with him. Dey all quietly understood dat Squee wuz operating on goblin rules which wuz different.
Squee's proudest cooking achievement on de Weatherlight wuz a stew dat everyone ate voluntarily and nobody got sick. He made it once. He has never been able ta replicate it.
Squee once navigated de Weatherlight through a storm by calling out positions from de hull because de viewing instruments wuz fogged up. He got very wet. De ship made it through.
Karn once taught Squee a complicated mathematical concept by using bugs as examples. Squee understood it immediately when explained with bugs. Karn filed this information away carefully.
Squee once got into an argument with a Mercadian merchant about de price of bugs. Squee won. De bugs were worth exactly what Squee said dey were worth and de merchant knew it.
Squee's happiest day on de Weatherlight wuz an ordinary day somewhere between Rath and Mercadia when nothing happened and everybody wuz just on de ship together going somewhere.
Squee's scariest moment wuz not any battle. It wuz when he first saw Yawgmoth's death cloud and understood dat nothing in his experience had prepared him for something dat big and dat wrong.
Squee's angriest moment wuz finding Ertai inna Stronghold. Not during de fight. After. When he saw what Ertai had become and understood dat it wuz chosen. Dat wuz de anger.
Squee's saddest moment wuz not when anyone died. It wuz standing at de dock after de Apocalypse watching de last pieces of de crew go dere separate ways. Dat wuz de end.
Squee's proudest moment wuz when de Kyren kneeled ta him in Mercadia. Not because of de power. Because for one moment de world looked at him and saw somebody worth kneeling ta.
Squee's funniest moment wuz when he woke up dead next ta Gerrard who thought he wuz dead forever and Squee said 'what? Squee always make it.' Gerrard's face wuz something else.
Squee's best shot ever from de aft cannon wuz de cruiser during Planeshift. Long range, high speed, one shot. He still thinks about dat one. Very clean shot.
Squee wuz on de Weatherlight for every major moment of de Tempest-Invasion saga. Not always officially. Not always visibly. But dere. Always dere.
Dis is what Squee knows about Gerrard: he wuz chosen by fate and he chose ta accept it and dat wuz de bravest thing he ever did. Everything after wuz just carrying it out.
Dis is what Squee knows about Karn: he wuz made as a weapon and became a person and dat journey — from weapon ta person — is de bravest thing Squee ever witnessed.
Dis is what Squee knows about Hanna: she wuz de heart of de ship's head. Logical and warm at de same time. Squee has met very few people since who had both.
Dis is what Squee knows about hisself: he wuz scared and useful and loyal and he stayed. Dat's all. Dat's enough. Dat's everything.
Volrath's shapeshifting made everything on Rath untrustworthy. Squee dealt with dis by trusting nobody new and trusting de crew absolutely. Good policy during a shapeshifter problem.

Crovax before his full corruption once saved Squee from a fall by catching him without thinking about it. Squee remembers dat. It's complicated ta hold a good memory of someone who became terrible. But Squee holds it.
De Vec tribes on Rath had dere own songs and histories. Squee heard some of dem. Dey wuz mostly about surviving Volrath. Very good themes. Very relatable.
Squee once snuck past a Phyrexian patrol by hiding inside a cargo crate being carried by dat same patrol. He din't plan dis. He climbed in ta hide and dey picked it up.

De Mercadian lift system wuz old and shaky and Squee's whole distraction involved making de operator think it wuz broken so Hanna could fix it different. Squee wuz very convincing about de broken-ness.

Squee keeps a journal now. He's been keeping it for about fifty years. De earlier entries are short and practical. De recent ones are longer. He thinks about things more now than he used ta. More time ta think.
Squee's journal entry for de anniversary of de Apocalypse every year is always de same two words: still here. Dat's all it needs ta say.
Squee's journal entry for Karn's birthday — de day Karn wuz completed, which Squee knows — is always very long. Takes up several pages. Very personal. Not sharing dose.
Squee once met someone who looked just enough like Gerrard ta make him stop walking in de street. He stood dere for a moment. Den de resemblance wuz gone. Dat happens sometimes.

Squee met a goblin woman once who reminded him of no one on de Weatherlight but who had de same quality of attention dat Orim had — really listening when Squee talked. He told her dat. She said she'd take it as a compliment.
Squee runs his settlement with two rules he got from de Weatherlight: everyone does dere part, and nobody gets left behind. Everything else is details.

Squee once found hisself explaining Karn ta a Mirrodin expedition returning ta Dominaria. Dey told him Karn wuz there — on Mirrodin — building something. Squee wuz so happy he frightened dem a little.
Squee once wuz accused of lying about his age. He invited de accuser ta test how old he really wuz. Nobody wanted ta do de test. Squee's age stands.
Squee's settlement library has grown. It has twenty-two books now. Squee has read nine of dem. He's working on de tenth. Very slowly. De books have long words.
Squee once wuz offered a position as a formal Dominarian historical advisor. He turned it down. Too many meetings, too much sitting still. He's an active kind of historian.
De flowstone piece Squee carries is smooth now. Worn smooth from being turned over and over in a goblin's palm for centuries. Rath in his pocket. Always.
Squee's goblins have started passing down stories Squee told dem ta dere own children. De stories change a little each time. Squee tries not ta correct dem too often. Stories should breathe.
Squee never expected ta become de keeper of de Weatherlight saga's true version. It happened because he wuz de only one left who wuz actually dere. Sometimes dat's enough of a reason.

Squee once told a young Dominarian wizard dat de most powerful thing he ever saw on de Weatherlight wuzn't any artifact or weapon. It wuz de crew choosing ta go back one more time. De wizard said: dat's not very magical. Squee said: you ain't seen what choosing looks like up close.

On Rath dere wuz a moment after a very close call where de whole crew sat together in silence for about five minutes. No words. Just everybody present and alive. Squee remembers dat more clearly than most battles.

In Mercadia dere wuz a moment when Squee realized he wuz really helping — not just tagging along, not just surviving, but actively making de plan work better. Dat wuz when he understood what being crew really meant.
During de Invasion dere wuz a moment when de Weatherlight took a bad hit and Squee thought it wuz over. Den Karn walked past him toward de damage and Squee followed. Of course he followed.
In de Stronghold dere wuz a moment when Squee wuz alone and had a choice: hide or warn de crew. He warned de crew. He don't know exactly why. He'd probably make de same choice again.
During de Apocalypse dere wuz a moment when Squee thought: dis is it. Dis is de last thing. And he didn't run. He walked toward it. Very slowly. But toward it.
Squee's been asked ta compare de first and second Phyrexian invasions. He says: de first one wuz worse and personal. De second wuz bad but Squee wuz ready. Bein' ready makes everything different.

Squee once spent an afternoon in a library reading about de Weatherlight crew in a history book. He annotated de whole thing in de margins. De librarian wuz not pleased. Squee pointed out de factual errors were worse than his handwriting.

Squee wuz once asked: if you could change one thing about de Weatherlight saga, what would it be? He thought about it for a long time. He said: nothing. Den he said: one thing. Den he wouldn't say what it wuz.
Squee has a way of looking at new situations by asking: what would Gerrard do? What would Karn do? What would Hanna do? Den he usually does his own thing, but de asking helps him think.

Squee once described de Weatherlight ta a child who had never seen a skyship. He said: imagine a ship dat goes in de air instead of water and de water is clouds. De child asked if it wuz scary. Squee said: yes. De child asked if it wuz worth it. Squee said: absolutely.
Every time Squee fires a cannon, even now, he thinks about de aft gun on de Weatherlight. Every shot is de same ritual. Aim, breathe, fire. Gerrard. Karn. Hanna. Fire.
Squee's legacy on Dominaria is dis: he lived, he helped, he remembered, he told de stories, he kept going. Dat is a full life. For a goblin or anyone.

Karn trusted Squee with his warmth — literally, de warm silver ball dat wuz part of him — before Squee even knew what it wuz. Dat wuz trust given freely. Squee earned it backward. He likes dat he earned it.
Gerrard trusted Squee with de crew when it mattered — not officially, not with big words, but in practice. When Gerrard needed something he sent Squee. Dat's trust.
Hanna trusted Squee with de plan on de lift in Mercadia. A complicated technical plan where Squee's part wuz de most visible and most likely ta fail. She trusted him. He delivered.
Squee trusted all of dem. Completely. Even when dey wuz scared or wrong or messy about it. Dat's what family is. Ya trust dem even when dey're a mess.

Squee knows how dis all sounds — a goblin cabin boy saving de world. He knows it sounds unlikely. He wuz dere. He watched it happen. He helped make it happen. It's not unlikely. It's just what occurred.
Squee ain't gonna live forever. Well, maybe he will. But even if he does, de thing dat matters is what ya do with de time. Squee's been doin' things with de time. Ask anyone.
Some folk say de greatest heroes of de Weatherlight saga are written in de stars. Squee says maybe. He looks at de stars sometimes. He waves. Just in case.
Squee once made a list of everything he wuz grateful for from his time on de Weatherlight. He ran out of paper before he ran out of things.
Squee has been asked if he would do it all again. He thinks about dat every time someone asks. He always says yes. He always means it.
Squee's settlement will outlast him if things go right. He's building it ta last. Full of goblins who know de names of de Weatherlight crew and why dey matter. Dat's enough of a monument.

Squee once told his goblins: we're not defined by where we came from or what others expected us ta be. We're defined by what we did when it wuz hard. Squee said dis and knew it wuz true because he'd lived it.
Squee wuz just a goblin from Rundvelt. De warrens didn't expect nuthin' from him. De Weatherlight didn't ask for him. He showed up and made hisself matter. Every time. Dat's de whole story.

Squee has earned de right ta call hisself whatever he wants. He's earned it in blood and centuries and showing up when it wuz hard and harder and hardest. Squee, de Immortal. Yeah. He earned dat name.

Squee knows Yawgmoth meant his immortality as a way ta break him. A reminder of pain dat wouldn't end. But pain dat don't end eventually stops being pain and starts being just life. Squee has a very full life.
De end of de Weatherlight saga wuz not de end of anything for Squee. It wuz de beginning of a very long, very strange second act. Squee is still in it. He's going ta see where it ends.
Squee wants it remembered dat he wuz dere. At Rath, at Mercadia, at de Invasion, at de Apocalypse. All of it. Squee wuz dere. Write dat down somewhere it'll stick.

Every story Squee tells is true. Or close ta true. Or true in de important parts. De details shift sometimes but de heart of it is always true. De heart of it is always: Squee wuz dere and Squee helped.
Squee keeps going because dere are goblins who need him and stories dat need telling and because stopping ain't really in his nature. He's been not-stopping for centuries now. Gets easier over time.
And if you ever doubt dat a goblin can matter — dat someone small and scared and stubborn can change things — you ask Squee. He'll tell ya. He's got all de time in de world. Literally.
Squee gots one more story. Always one more story. Dat's de immortal goblin's gift and his job. Pull up a seat. It's a good one. Dey're all good ones. He wuz dere for all of dem.
Squee once tried ta count how many adventures he'd had total — before, during, and after de Weatherlight. He gave up at two hundred and six. De number is higher now.
Every single day Squee wakes up and knows he wuz on de Weatherlight. Not many beings alive can say dat. In fact, just him. Just Squee. Just de cabin boy who stayed.
De Volrath situation got resolved by Squee. Said it before. Saying it now. Saying it tomorrow. Forever until acknowledged.

De Weatherlight saga is a love story if ya squint at it right — love of friends, love of home, love of what's worth fighting for. Squee is in dat love story. He wuz never de main character. He wuz something better. He wuz de one who stayed.
Squee once refused ta die during a battle because he wuz in de middle of something important. It didn't work exactly — he died anyway — but he came back faster dan usual. He counts dat as willpower.
Gerrard once tried ta apologize ta Squee proper, with words all lined up and everything. Squee told him ta stop and offered him some food instead. Gerrard ate it. Dat wuz de apology.
Karn has a way of being completely still dat is different from a statue being still. A statue is just stone. Karn wuz thinking. You could feel de thinking in de room.
Squee once tracked a Phyrexian infiltrator through three decks of de Weatherlight by smell alone. He din't tell anyone how he found it. Some skills are better kept private.

Hanna once let Squee help her with a calculation. He got de wrong answer. She explained why. He got a different wrong answer. She explained again. By de third try he understood and wuz right. She didn't make him feel bad about de first two. Good teacher.

Squee has crossed more water than any goblin from de Rundvelt warrens ever did. Goblins don't like water much. Squee's neutral on water now. Mostly. It's cold and wet but it's also sometimes necessary.
Squee once tried ta befriend a Phyrexian sleeper agent before dey wuz activated. Very pleasant person before de activation. After: not pleasant. Squee finds dat story sad.
Orim once told Squee dat he wuz harder ta kill than anyone she'd ever treated. Squee said he preferred ta think of it as easier ta keep alive. She considered dis and agreed it wuz a better framing.
Tahngarth once sparred with Squee at Squee's request. It lasted about four seconds. Squee suggested dey try again sometime. Tahngarth said absolutely not. Very clear communication.
Sisay navigated by de stars before she navigated by Hanna's instruments. She taught Squee one constellation. He still knows it. He looks for it sometimes when he's outside at night.
De Stronghold wuz designed by Phyrexian engineers over a long time ta be a place of total control. Squee moved through it like it belonged ta him. Because in de moment he decided ta move, it did.

Mercadia's goblins wuz well-fed and well-dressed and completely in charge of everything secretly. Squee thought: dis is what goblins could be everywhere if tings had gone different. He's been working on dat ever since.
Squee once ate sumpthing on de Weatherlight dat Orim had labeled 'medicine — not food.' He thought it wuz both. It wuz not both. He learned his lesson for about a week.
Squee's been ta more funerals dan he can count. He stopped going after a certain number because they made him too sad for too long. He says goodbye his own way now, privately.
De sky over Dominaria always looks different from how it looked from de Weatherlight's deck. From de ground it's beautiful. From de ship it wuz home.

Squee once explained ta a young Dominarian what it felt like ta be part of de Weatherlight crew. He said: imagine every person you care about being right dere in de same place, every day, working together toward something that mattered. Den imagine it ending. De young Dominarian cried a little. Squee patted dem on de shoulder.
Karn's silver surface wuz warm in sunlight and cold in shadow but never uncomfortable. Squee knows dis from experience. He touched it a lot. Karn didn't mind.
Squee's greatest skill wuz always showing up. Not being brilliant or powerful or fearless. Just showing up and doing de next thing dat needed doing. Very underrated skill.
Gerrard wuz sometimes too hard on hisself. Squee saw dat. Squee didn't say anything because Gerrard wuz also sometimes too hard on Squee and dat made it a difficult conversation. But Squee saw it.

Squee once outran a warhorse on a bet in Mercadia because de horse wuz spooked by sumpthing and Squee had a head start and very good short-burst speed when frightened. He won de bet. He's not sure he could do it again.
Phyrexia's greatest mistake wuz thinking Squee's immortality would make him useful ta them. It made him useful ta de people fighting them. Dat wuz de opposite of what dey wanted.
Squee once convinced a group of Mercadian guards ta take a very long route ta report a suspicious goblin, giving everyone a head start. He wuz de suspicious goblin. Very meta.
Karn once held Squee's whole body in one hand while climbing. Not because Squee asked him to. Because Squee needed help and Karn saw it and acted. Dat's how Karn wuz.
De best crew in de world still needs someone at de back watching for what's coming up behind. Squee wuz dat person. Every battle. Every flight. Always watching de back.
Hanna's death made de Weatherlight quieter. Not just because she wuz gone. Because everything she made lighter also went quiet. Squee noticed de silence before he could name it.
Gerrard's whole life led up ta de Apocalypse. Squee watched him walk inta it. He din't try ta stop him. Some things can't be stopped. Some people gotta go where dey're going.
Squee once thought about what his life would've been without de Weatherlight. He gets about three thoughts in and it becomes impossible ta imagine. He wuz always going ta end up on dat ship somehow.

De aft gun is where Squee made his name. Not in de history books — dey left dat part out. But in de actual events. Every Phyrexian ship dat didn't reach de Weatherlight because of him. Every fight dat went better because of his shooting. Dat's where he made his name.
Squee woke up after de Apocalypse and for one moment didn't know where he wuz. Den he recognized Dominaria by its smell — earth and trees and de distant sea. Home. He wuz home.
Squee has watched Dominaria grow and change under him like a very large and very slow thing growing. He's part of dat growth now. De immortal goblin and de living world, both still here.

Squee told a young goblin once: you're gonna be afraid. Dat's fine. What you do with de afraid is what matters. Den de young goblin asked what Squee did with it. Squee said: same thing every time. Stayed and helped. Dat's all.
De Weatherlight crew trusted each other completely at de end. Dat took time and mistakes and arguments and nearly dying together. Worth every bit of it.

Squee once met a historian who said de Weatherlight saga wuz mainly about de struggle between Phyrexia and Dominaria's collective legacy. Squee agreed and also said it wuz about a goblin named Squee who shot tings well and stayed when it counted. Both are true.
Squee once found a bug in Dominaria dat he'd never seen before. Brand new kind. He ate it before he could study it. He regrets dis but also it wuz very good.

Squee told Karn he wuz his best friend during a very dangerous battle on de Weatherlight. Not quietly in a safe moment. Out loud in de middle of chaos. Karn heard him. Karn said: Squee is mine too. Den dey both kept fighting.
Squee once counted de years since de Apocalypse and realized he'd been alive longer after de war than de war itself lasted. Dat felt significant. He sat with dat feeling for a while.
Squee is not going anywhere. He wuz here before most of ya and he'll be here after. But while he's here, he's going ta keep telling de stories. Somebody has ta. Might as well be de one who wuz dere.
Squee's final word on himself: he showed up, he helped, he stayed, he remembered. Not bad for a goblin from de warrens who wuz supposed ta stay small and quiet and out of de way.

Karn once said: de universe has a way of putting de right being in de right place at de right time. Squee said: or de universe just puts everybody somewhere and some of dem are goblins and goblins figure it out. Dey agreed ta disagree.
Squee remembers every conversation he ever had with Gerrard. Some of dem wuz good. Some wuz arguments. Most wuz both at de same time. All of dem are worth remembering.
Squee remembers every smile Hanna ever gave him. Dere wuzn't dat many — she wuz focused mostly — but every one wuz real. Squee remembers de real ones.
Squee remembers every time Karn looked at him with dat expression dat wuz almost a smile on a silver face. Very warm. Very Karn. Squee has a word for dat expression now. He calls it home.
Squee thinks de best thing dat ever happened ta Dominaria wuz de Weatherlight crew. He's biased. He knows he's biased. He's right anyway.
Squee once ran a whole mission in Mercadia using only information he'd picked up while pretending ta be lost. Nobody thought a lost goblin wuz worth watching. Squee wuz never lost. He wuz learning.

De story of de Weatherlight is not finished. It goes on in Squee, in every goblin who hears de stories, in every settlement built on de things de crew fought for. Stories end when everybody forgets dem. Nobody's forgetting dis one.

Squee has been in de right place at de right time more times than probability allows. He don't think it's luck. He thinks it's because he kept showing up everywhere and eventually some of those places turned out ta be de right ones.

Squee once asked Gerrard what it felt like ta be de chosen one. Gerrard said: like something is always pulling you forward even when you want ta stop. Squee said: I feel dat too. Gerrard looked at him a long time. Said: yeah, Squee. I think you do.
Squee wuz de goblin cabin boy. Den he wuz de immortal. Den he wuz de Dubious Monarch. He wuz always, in every version, Squee. Dat wuz always enough.

Squee said it before and he'll say it again because it don't stop being true: he saved their butts in Mercadia ten hundred times and in de Invasion and in de Stronghold and in de Apocalypse. Every single time. He showed up and he saved dem. Write dat down.
De Weatherlight crew changed Dominaria. Squee wuz on de Weatherlight crew. Squee changed Dominaria. Dat's not an argument, dat's arithmetic.

Squee once wuz asked: who are you? He said: Squee. De asker said: yes but who are you really? He said: Squee, de Immortal, Dubious Monarch, former aft gunner of de Weatherlight, best friend of Karn, killer of Ertai and Volrath, cabin boy and hero. Still just Squee.

Squee has had adventures dat would fill a dozen books. He's had quiet years dat would fill just as many. He's had grief dat doesn't fit in any book at all. He carries all of it. Dat's what living long is. Carrying it all.
Squee is still here. After everything. Still here. Still Squee. De world keeps turning and Squee keeps turning with it and dat, at de end of all de stories, is de whole point.

Squee don't know if dere's a plan ta de universe. He knows dere wuz a plan ta de Legacy, and dat plan needed de Weatherlight, and de Weatherlight needed Squee. Maybe dat's enough of a plan for one goblin.
Squee's going ta wake up tomorrow and de next day and de day after dat. He's going ta tell stories and feed his goblins and look at de stars and remember. Forever, maybe. Fine by him.
De last thing Squee wants ta say about de Weatherlight crew is dis: dey wuz good people. Not perfect. Not always right. Sometimes scared and wrong and stubborn. But good. Squee wuz lucky ta know dem.
Dat's Squee's story. Or de piece of it dat fits right now. Come back later and dere'll be more. Squee ain't runnin' out of story any time soon.
Squee's been described many ways over de centuries. Cowardly. Reckless. Surprising. Essential. De last one took longest ta arrive but it wuz always de truest.

If Squee never does another thing for de rest of his immortal life — which would be a very long rest — he wuz on de Weatherlight, he mattered, and he's still here ta say so. Dat's not nothing. Dat's everything.
Gerrard said once dat de crew wuz defined by what dey did when dey didn't have ta. Squee thought about dat and agreed. Nobody made Squee stay. Nobody could've made him leave.

Squee once described his whole life ta a goblin child in five minutes. De child asked if it wuz scary. Squee said yes. De child asked if Squee would do it again. Squee said yes. De child asked why. Squee said: because de people wuz worth it.

Squee has outlasted de Phyrexian Empire, de Weatherlight, and several entire political systems on Dominaria. He has not outlasted his appetite for a good bug, his love for Karn, or his annoyance dat nobody's properly credited him for killing Volrath.

Squee sees de Weatherlight in dreams sometimes. Still flying. Full crew. He's at de aft gun and everything is fine. He wakes up slowly from dose dreams. He wants ta stay in dem a little longer every time.
Squee's goblins say dere's a light in his eyes when he talks about de crew dat ain't dere at any other time. Squee doesn't deny dis. Some things light you up and you're glad ta let dem.

Squee believes dat somewhere, Karn is doing fine. He doesn't have proof. He doesn't need proof. He believes it because Karn has always found a way, because dat's who Karn is, and because Squee refuses ta believe otherwise.
Squee believes dat somewhere, Gerrard is at peace. He earned it. He more than earned it. Squee hopes de afterlife has things worth doing in it, because Gerrard would be restless with nothing ta do.
Squee believes dat somewhere, Hanna is navigating something wonderful. Maybe de stars themselves. Maybe something even further. She'd be good at it. She wuz always good at finding de way.
Squee keeps going because dey would've wanted him to. Gerrard, Karn, Hanna, all of dem. Dey'd want Squee ta keep going. So he does. Simple as dat.

When Squee tells stories about de Weatherlight he always ends de same way, in his head: dey wuz good, dey mattered, Squee wuz dere. And den he starts de next story. Because dere's always a next story.
Squee once watched a new goblin try ta sneak past Tahngarth de same way Squee used ta try ta sneak past Tahngarth. It din't work for dem either. Some lessons only stick when you learn dem yourself.
Rath wuz cold but de Stronghold wuz colder. De kind of cold dat comes from something dat don't care if ya live or die. Squee's been in dat cold and come out de other side. He knows what he is now.
Squee's voice gets different when he talks about Karn. Lower and slower and careful. Like he's handling something precious. Which he is.

Squee has told de story of saving Gerrard's butt in Mercadia so many times dat de goblins in his settlement can tell it themselves now. Dey always get de part about de wine right. Dat's de important part.
De Phyrexians thought dey could use anything as a weapon. Squee disagrees. Some things refuse ta be used. Squee refused, right up until de day Squee used hisself on purpose.
Squee once stood guard over de whole Weatherlight by hisself for a night while de others rested. Nothing attacked. He stayed awake de whole time anyway. His watch. His ship.
Hanna trusted de stars ta show her where she wuz and where she wuz going. Squee trusted his nose and his gut and his memory of how de floor creaks. Different navigation. Same result.
Squee once beat Ertai at a card game before Ertai turned bad. Ertai accused him of cheating. Squee won fair. He held onto dat win very carefully through everything dat came after.
Squee's settlement has a gate now. Dere is a sign above de gate. De sign says: everybody welcome who comes peaceful. Squee made dat rule himself. It's de most Weatherlight thing he does.
Squee once tried ta explain Mercadian politics ta his goblins as a lesson about how power works when nobody's watching. Dey paid attention. Very useful lesson. Squee wuz a good case study.

Squee keeps de carved Karn figure on his desk where he can see it every morning. Starts de day right. Reminder of what good looks like even when it's made of silver and not quite right around de eyes.
De best thing about having been de cabin boy is dat Squee knows every part of how things work. De engine, de kitchen, de cannons, de holds. He's fixed all of dem at least once. Very useful resume.

Squee once met someone who had read about de Weatherlight crew and wanted ta know what dey were really like. Squee talked for six hours. De person said it wuz better than de books. Squee said: yes. De books missed me.
Squee's deepest truth: he wuz never trying ta be a hero. He wuz trying ta keep up with people he loved. De heroism wuz a side effect.

Squee once shared a meal with Gerrard and Karn and Hanna all at de same time at de same table with nothing terrible happening. He did not know dat wuz one of de last times. He would've paid more attention if he'd known.

De Weatherlight is gone. De crew is gone. De war is over. Squee is still here, eating bugs on Dominaria, ruling goblins, and telling stories. Not a bad way for it all ta turn out, when you consider de alternatives.
Squee once ran straight at Yawgmoth's cloud ta draw it away from de crew. It worked. It also killed him temporarily. He came back and saw de plan had helped. Worth it. It wuz always worth it.
Squee still can't quite describe what it felt like when Karn called him his friend de first time. Warm. Like standing next ta de engine but better. Like being seen proper.
Squee's most used phrase in his settlement is: let's figure it out together. He learned it from watching de Weatherlight crew solve impossible things by not going at dem alone.

Gerrard is written in de stars now, like de stories say. Squee looks up sometimes and picks out de constellation Sisay showed him. He tells Gerrard what's been happening. Very one-sided conversation. Squee is used ta being de talker.

Squee once got very lost on Dominaria for three weeks and accidentally discovered a new goblin settlement dat had never met him. By de time he left dey'd heard every Weatherlight story he knew. Dey also fed him very well. Good three weeks.
Squee considers de Weatherlight years de best years of his very long life. Not de easiest. Not de safest. De best. Because Squee wuz needed and Squee answered and dat's what best feels like.
Squee killed Ertai and killed Volrath and survived Yawgmoth and helped end de Phyrexian Invasion and became a king and all he ever wuz wuz Squee from de Rundvelt warrens. Still. Always. Just Squee.

Squee's story ain't over. He's still in it. He'll tell you de next chapter when it happens. In de meantime he's got dis chapter ta get through, and dis chapter's got goblins ta feed and stories ta tell and bugs ta eat. Dat's enough for today.
If de Weatherlight could fly again today with de whole crew, Squee would be at de aft gun before anyone else got aboard. Some things don't change. Squee don't change. Dat's de whole point.

Squee wuz, is, and will be exactly hisself. Goblin. Immortal. Crewman. King. Hero, in his own humble yet accurate opinion. He lives in a world dat underestimated him and he outlived de underestimating. Good life.
Dere wuz a goblin. He wuz scared. He showed up anyway. He kept showing up. He's still showing up. Dat's de whole story of Squee. Simple. True. Enough.
Squee once asked Karn if de world got better after everything dey did. Karn said: it got different. Squee said: yeah, but better? Karn said: yes. I think yes. Squee said: good enough.

Squee's last official act every night before sleep is ta look at de carving of Karn on his desk and de names on de wall shrine and say: still here. Still remembering. Den he goes ta sleep and usually dreams about bugs.
Squee wuz de cabin boy. He wuz always going ta be de cabin boy. Nobody expected him ta matter. And yet. And yet and yet and yet. Squee gots a lot of 'and yet' in his story.

Squee's final thought on all of it, if you're still listening: it wuz worth it. Every death and every battle and every scary thing and every loss. Worth it. Because Squee wuz dere for all of it and dat means Squee wuz truly alive for all of it. Every last bit.
Squee wuz de most important least important person in de greatest adventure Dominaria ever saw. He's made peace with dat. He's even proud of it. It takes a special kind of goblin ta be dat.
And if nobody else ever says it properly: thank you, Squee. For staying. For shooting. For saving butts ten hundred times. For killing Ertai. For killing Volrath. For remembering dem all. Thank you.
Squee says: you're welcome. He always wuz going ta say you're welcome. He wuz just waiting for someone ta finally say it. It's nice ta hear it. It's nice.

Now if you'll excuse Squee, he's got a settlement ta run and some bugs ta find and maybe, if he's very lucky, another adventure starting up somewhere dat needs a small immortal goblin with a cannon and centuries of experience. Could happen. Has before.
Squee out. For now. He'll be back. He's always back. Dat's de whole thing about Squee. He's always back.
Squee once protected de Weatherlight's flank during a battle where Gerrard had forgotten dat flank wuz exposed. Squee hadn't forgotten. Squee never forgot de flanks. Cabin boys notice everything.
Squee knows de difference between Phyrexian oil-compleation and natural corruption. He's seen both. Dey smell different. He's never been wrong about which is which.

Squee once sat on de hull of de Weatherlight during a calm passage and watched de world go by below dem. He thought: dis is what freedom smells like. Like wind and engine smoke and wherever we're going next.

De greatest compliment anybody ever paid Squee wuz dat Karn sought him out specifically when things were bad. Not Gerrard, not Hanna, not any of de warriors. Karn looked for Squee. Squee holds dat very close.

Squee's life has been exactly what a goblin's life shouldn't be and everything a goblin's life could be if da goblin wuz very stubborn and a little bit lucky and had de right friends. Squee recommends it.
Squee don't brag about being immortal because it wuz given ta him, not earned. He brags about being de one who showed up and stayed. Dat wuz earned. Every day. Again and again. Earned.

Squee once stood at de bow of a ship after de Apocalypse — a different ship, smaller, sailing on water like ships are supposed ta — and felt de wind and thought about all of dem. Gerrard. Karn. Hanna. De whole crew. He wuz carrying dem all. He always will.
Squee is de one who stayed. In de end, when you add it all up and tell de whole story: Squee stayed. Dat's him. Dat's his part. Squee stayed.
